comments.body: Can you please provide a sample repository where the same thing happens?
Hey thanks for giving the answer, this method does work perfectly. But really I have no idea why the deleteBy isn't doing anything.

answers.body:
Try replacing the call with an explicit find + deleteAll:

List<Embedding> embeddings = repository.findAllByFileName(fileName);
log.info("Deleting {} Embedding objects with fileName={}", embeddings.size(), fileName);
repository.deleteAll(embeddings);

Why?

Spring Data Redis retrieves IDs via the @Indexed field and then deletes by those IDs — if the index is broken, outdated, or Redis fails to deserialize the objects, the deletion may silently do nothing. An explicit find → deleteAll is easier to debug and log.

title: Spring Data Redis Repository Function delete...By not working?
body:
I am using spring-data-redis (version 3.5.0-M2).

This is my entity class:

@RedisHash("Embedding")
@Data
@NoArgsConstructor
@AllArgsConstructor
public class Embedding {

    @Id
    private String id;

    private byte[] embedding;
    private String text;
    private int segmentIndex;

    @Indexed
    private String fileName;

    private Instant createTs;

    @Transient
    private Double score;

    public Embedding(float[] embedding, String text, int segmentIndex, String fileName) {
        this.embedding = RedisConversionUtil.floatsToBytes(embedding);
        this.text = text;
        this.segmentIndex = segmentIndex;
        this.fileName = fileName;
        this.createTs = Instant.now();
    }

    public float[] getEmbedding() {
        return RedisConversionUtil.bytesToFloats(embedding);
    }

    public byte[] getRawEmbedding() {
        return embedding;
    }
}

This is my repository class:

public interface EmbeddingRepository extends ListCrudRepository<Embedding, String>, CustomEmbeddingRepository {
    
    void deleteAllByFileName(String fileName);
}

The problem is that deleteAllByFileName() method is not doing anything when executed (deleteAll() works although). I referred to the spring documentation here. In table 1, delete…By() is mentioned to be supported. What am I missing here?
